description: Missing `description` frontmatter field (only `model: haiku` present)

Both declare `name: time-agent` with different implementations (PKT/UTC+5 vs Dubai GST/UTC+4)
`npx -y @playwright/mcp` — no version pin
suggested_fix: Change to `npx -y @playwright/mcp@0.0.29` (or current stable); repeat for all three servers

description: `npx -y deepwiki-mcp` — unknown package, no pin
suggested_fix: Audit package provenance; pin to a specific version; consider replacing with a more established alternative if available

description: `subprocess.Popen` resolves audio player from PATH
suggested_fix: Acceptable risk given current sanitization; optionally hardcode known safe paths (e.g., `/usr/bin/afplay`) instead of relying on PATH resolution

description: Hook log may persist sensitive tool input data
suggested_fix: Add `hooks-log.jsonl` to `.gitignore`; consider scrubbing `tool_input` from log entries before writing

description: Zero usage examples across every agent in the repo — systematic gap
Body explicitly says "read-only research, Do NOT modify any local files" but `allowedTools` includes `Write`, `Edit`, `Agent`, `NotebookEdit`, `mcp__*`
Body says "not to write files or create outputs" but `allowedTools` includes `Write`, `Edit`, `NotebookEdit`
Single-purpose time agent (runs one bash date command) declares 10+ tools: `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `Agent`, `NotebookEdit`, `mcp__*` — nearly all unused
All five workflow research agents declare `Write` and `Edit` in `allowedTools` despite each stating "This is a read-only research workflow. Do NOT modify any files."
`NotebookEdit` in allowedTools is not used by HTML-presentation-editing agents
